author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
ore mana! dhana⋅jana⋅jīvana⋅yauvana
ei āche ei nāi, cakṣe kinā dekha tāi,
tumi kise balicha āpana

niśira svapana jena, e dhana⋅sampada tena,
tileke sakali bhāi miche
dekhiyā nā dekha kene, śuniyā nā śuna kāṇe,
ki lāgi chāḍite nāra icche

kanyā putra jata ithi, marile se jāya kathi,
ki jāni kothāya tumi jāo
mithyā ‘mora mora’ kara, rātridina bhāvi’ mara,
para lāgi’ āpanā hārāo

kibā āra anya para, āpanā e kalevara,
se nāhi tomāra saṅge jāya
pāchu nāhi dekha evā, tora lāgi kānde kebā,
kāra lāgi kara hāya hāya

jebā hoiyāche āyu, se mātra nāsāra vāyu,
sariyā paḍile āra nāi
kibā vṛddha kibā vāla, nāhi tāra kālākāla,
kothā thāke jauvana⋅baḍāi

e⋅sakala jā̐ra māyā, tā̐re kena bhula bhāyā,
jā̐ra nāme tribhuvana tare
premānanda kahe jadi, ‘kṛṣṇa’ kaha niravadhi,
tabe ki e jana āra mare
en_translation:
O my mind! Wealth, family, life, and youth—they are here one moment and gone the next. Do you not see this with your own eyes? How then can you call them your own?

These wealth and possessions are just like a dream in the night. In an instant, O brother, it is all revealed to be false. Why do you see yet remain blind? Why do you hear yet remain deaf? For what reason can you not abandon your worldly desires?

All your children—where will they go when you die? And who knows where you yourself will go? Falsely you say "mine, mine," and you worry and die in such thoughts. For the sake of others, you lose yourself.

What to speak of others? Even this very body of yours does not go with you. Do you not see what remains behind? Who will truly cry for you? For whom do you lament so much?

Whatever life you have is merely the breath in your nostrils. Once it leaves, it is no more. Whether you are old or young, there is no fixed time for death. Where then remains the pride of youth?

All of this is the illusion of Lord Kṛṣṇa. Why do you forget Him, O brother? By His Name, the three worlds are delivered. Premānanda says: "If you chant the Name of Kṛṣṇa constantly, then how can you ever really die?"
